title: Wire tantivy into GET /api/v2/search?type=statuses
description:
## What
Implement the statuses branch of GET /api/v2/search by executing a tantivy BooleanQuery and returning matching Status objects, with visibility filtering and query sanitization.

## Why
Search is unusable until it is exposed via the Mastodon API endpoint that clients call.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: curl mastodon.social /api/v2/search?q=test&type=statuses to capture the exact JSON response shape ({accounts:[], statuses:[], hashtags:[]}); note that statuses is an array of Status objects (same shape as timeline); check resolve=false behavior (no remote fetch for statuses); note offset and limit params; confirm max results returned by mastodon.social.
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: in the /api/v2/search handler, when type=statuses, sanitize the query string (strip tantivy special chars: +, -, &&, ||, !, (, ), {, }, [, ], ^, ", ~, *, ?, :, \; cap at 500 chars); apply tokio::time::timeout of 5 seconds around the entire search path; build a tantivy BooleanQuery with TermQuery per whitespace-split word against the content and spoiler_text fields; filter by visibility: (visibility = "public") OR (persona_id = authed_persona_id); collect top 40 results ordered by score; extract stored id field from each result; fetch Status objects from DB by those ids in order; return {accounts:[], statuses:[...], hashtags:[]} JSON with status 200.
- Spawn a Test subagent to: verify response shape against mastodon.social /api/v2/search fixture JSON; test that a public post by another persona appears in search; test that a followers-only post by another persona does NOT appear; test that own followers-only post DOES appear; test query with tantivy special chars is not rejected with 500.

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: mastodon.social /api/v2/search?q=test&type=statuses live fixture for response shape and field names.
- Test approach: seed DB with known posts, execute search query, assert returned status ids match expected set; use fixture JSON from mastodon.social to validate serialization field names and types.

## Defensive programming checklist
- No `.unwrap()` in handler paths
- Sanitize query string before passing to tantivy query parser — strip all tantivy special chars to prevent query injection
- Cap query length at 500 chars; return empty results (not 400) for empty query after sanitization
- Wrap entire search in tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(5)) — on timeout return empty results with 200, not 500
- Only return public posts and own posts — never expose another persona's followers-only or direct posts
- resolve=false must be respected — no outbound HTTP requests for status search
- Search results are ordered by tantivy score (relevance), not by created_at — this is correct and matches mastodon.social behavior
